title: Boston Marathon runner aims to complete marathons in all 50 states
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Zara Harding, 46, from Nevada, will run the Boston Marathon, aiming to complete marathons in all 50 states.
Harding has completed over a dozen marathons after colorectal cancer treatment; only 10 states remain for her goal.
Running for the Dana-Farber Team, Harding aims to raise $12,000 for cancer research through her marathon journey.

title: Judge dismisses lawsuit of Babson College student mistakenly deported to Honduras
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Judge Richard Stearns dismissed Babson student Any Lucia Lopez Belloza's lawsuit after her deportation to Honduras.
Lopez Belloza declined a flight back to the U.S., removing the court's jurisdiction in her case.
ICE's removal frustrated legal counsel, but the judge found no concealment of her location by the government.

title: NBA coach dubs Derrick White as a ‘top-5 player’ in NBA
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Cavaliers coach Kenny Atkinson praised Celtics' Derrick White as a top-5 player analytically in the NBA.
White averaged 17.4 points, 4.4 rebounds, 5.7 assists over the 2025-26 season, stepping up in Tatum's absence.
In Boston's 109-98 win over Cavaliers, White had 6 points, 7 rebounds, 5 assists; Brown and Tatum combined for 43 points.

title: Jaylen Brown, Jayson Tatum dynamic shines in Celtics win over Cavaliers: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Jaylen Brown scored 23 points, leading the Celtics to a 109-98 victory over the Cleveland Cavaliers.
Jayson Tatum's return boosts Celtics; he scored 20 points despite not being fully recovered.
Celtics’ elite defense limited the Cavaliers to just 10 points in the second quarter.

title: Former Patriots WR cautions against signing Alec Pierce to a hefty contract
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Alec Pierce, a free-agent receiver, led the NFL in yards per catch in 2024 and 2025 with the Colts.
Former Patriots WR Chris Hogan advises against paying Pierce $30 million for his deep threat capability alone.
The Patriots could sign Pierce due to cap space, but also have multiple roster upgrades needed this offseason.

title: North Kingstown, R.I. police officer dies by suicide, department says
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: North Kingstown Police officer Brittany Dougal died by suicide on March 4 after battling mental illness, the department said.
Dougal, 29, graduated second in her class from the Rhode Island Municipal Police Training Academy and joined the force in December.
A funeral in Danvers will honor Dougal, with donations suggested to the North Kingstown Animal Shelter and Tunnel to Towers Foundation.

title: 9 under-the-radar players for Patriots to consider in free agency
author: Conor Roche
bullets: The Patriots have $60 million in cap space for free agents this offseason.
Potential free agent targets include Jalen Nailor, Daniel Bellinger, and Teven Jenkins.
Linebacker Devin Bush and EDGE players Joseph Ossai and Arden Key are also suggested targets.

title: Did Tom Brady prevent Patriots from acquiring Maxx Crosby? One report suggests possibility.
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Tom Brady, a minority owner of the Raiders, may have influenced the decision to trade Maxx Crosby to the Ravens.
The Raiders traded Maxx Crosby to the Ravens for two first-round picks, including the 14th overall pick in 2026.
The Patriots missed out on Crosby and may pursue free-agent edge rushers like Jaelan Phillips and Boye Mafe.

title: Rumored Patriots target Alec Pierce reportedly expected to make at least $27M per year, might not hit free agency
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Alec Pierce, rumored Patriots target, expected to earn at least $27 million annually, complicating free agency prospects.
Colts make a push to re-sign wide receiver Alec Pierce, entering negotiations before legal tampering begins Monday.
Pierce, 25, led NFL in yards per reception past two seasons, logging 47 catches for 1,003 yards in 2025.

title: ‘Reading felt impossible’: Why she’s running the Boston Marathon for BPL
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Marguerite Flynn, from Hingham, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon supporting the Boston Public Library.
Flynn, who has dyslexia and ADHD, views libraries as crucial support systems in her academic journey.
Despite a rare vascular condition and multiple leg surgeries, Flynn is inspired to tackle the marathon challenge.
